document: Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025 (India - Row 4)

sections.heading: संडक्षप्त िाम और प्रारंभ — (1) इि डियमों को डिडिटल वैजयजिक िेटा संरक्षण डियमावली, 2025 कहा िाए।
sections.title: State use of personal data for sovereignty and security
sections.summary: This section permits the State or its instrumentalities to use personal data of a Data Principal for the sovereignty and integrity of India or security of the State. It designates specific officers of the State or instrumentalities as authorized persons to carry out this use, with the Central Government or head of the instrumentalities designated as the authority.

requirements.text: Use of personal data of a Data Principal in the interest of sovereignty and integrity of India or security of the State

Such officer of the State or of any of its instrumentalities notified under clause (a) of sub-section (2) of section 17 of the Act, as the Central Government or the head of such instrumentalities, as the case may be, may designate in this behalf

sections.source_quote: 1. Use, by the State or any of its instrumentalities, of personal data of a Data Principal in the interest of sovereignty and integrity of India or security of the State. Such officer of the State or of any of its instrumentalities notified under clause (a) of sub-section (2) of section 17 of the Act, as the Central Government or the head of such instrumentalities, as the case may be, may designate in this behalf.
sections.relevance_reason: State use of personal data for sovereignty/security

sections.heading: पररभार्ाएँ :—(1) इि डियमों में, िब तक दक संिभग के अिुसार अन्यथा अपेडक्षत ि हो, -
sections.title: State use of personal data for legal functions
sections.summary: This section permits the State or its instrumentalities to use personal data of a Data Principal for performing functions under any law in India or fulfilling obligations under any law in India. The authorized persons are those designated under applicable law.

requirements.text: Use of personal data of a Data Principal for performance of any function under any law for the time being in force in India
Use of personal data of a Data Principal for disclosure of any information for fulfilling any obligation under any law for the time being in force in India
Person authorized under applicable law

sections.source_quote: 2. Use, by the State or any of its instrumentalities, of personal data of a Data Principal for the following purposes, namely: — (i) performance of any function under any law for the time being in force in India; or (ii) disclosure of any information for fulfilling any obligation under any law for the time being in force in India. Person authorised under applicable law.
sections.relevance_reason: State use of personal data for legal functions

sections.heading: िेटा द़िड्युसरी द्वारा िेटा प्प्रंडसपल को दिया गया िोरिस — िेटा द़िड्युसरी द्वारा िेटा प्प्रंडसपल को दियि गया
sections.title: Assessment for significant data fiduciaries
sections.summary: This section requires the Central Government, specifically the Secretary in charge of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, to assess and notify any Data Fiduciary or class of Data Fiduciaries as Significant Data Fiduciaries. The assessment is conducted by designated officers within the Ministry.

requirements.text: Carrying out assessment for notifying any Data Fiduciary or class of Data Fiduciaries as Significant Data Fiduciary
Such officer of the Central Government, in the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, as the Secretary in charge of the said Ministry may designate in this behalf

sections.source_quote: 3. Carrying out assessment for notifying any Data Fiduciary or class of Data Fiduciaries as Significant Data Fiduciary. Such officer of the Central Government, in the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, as the Secretary in charge of the said Ministry may designate in this behalf.
sections.relevance_reason: Core: Significant Data Fiduciary assessment for cross-border data transfers

sections.title: Medical assistance for Board employees
sections.summary: Board officers and employees are entitled to medical assistance under group health insurance schemes approved by the Central Government, covering themselves and eligible dependants.

requirements.text: Officers and employees entitled to medical assistance as may be admissible under group health insurance scheme of the Board
Medical assistance made with previous approval of Central Government

sections.source_quote: 4. Medical assistance. —The officers and employees shall be entitled to such medical assistance as may be admissible to them and their eligible dependants under any group health insurance scheme of the Board, made with the previous approval of the Central Government.
sections.relevance_reason: medical assistance unrelated to data protection

sections.heading: राज्य और उसके उपकरणों द्वारा सडब्सिी, लाभ, सेवा, प्रमाण पत्र, लाइसेंस या परडमट के प्राविाि या िारी करिे के
sections.title: Leave entitlements for Board employees
sections.summary: Board officers and employees may avail leaves admissible to Central Government servants under the Central Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1972, including encashment of earned leave. Casual leave entitlements follow Central Government instructions.

requirements.text: Officers and employees may avail of leaves admissible to Government servant under Central Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1972
Officers and employees eligible for encashment of earned leave as provided in Central Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1972
Officers and employees entitled to casual leave to extent admissible to Government servant under Central Government instructions

sections.source_quote: 5. Leave. — (1) The officers and employees may avail of such kinds of leaves as are admissible to a Government servant under the Central Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1972, subject to the conditions applicable under the said rules, and shall be eligible for encashment of earned leave as provided therein. (2) The officers and employees shall be entitled to casual leave to such extent as is admissible to a Government servant under instructions issued by the Central Government.
sections.relevance_reason: Leave entitlements unrelated to data compliance

sections.title: Leave travel concession eligibility for civil servants
sections.summary: This section states that leave travel concession is admissible to officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1, following the Central Civil Services (Leave Travel Concession) Rules, 1988. These individuals are those appointed to civil services and posts connected to the Union of India's affairs. The concession is governed by specific rules for civil servants.

requirements.text: Leave travel concession shall be admissible to officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1

sections.source_quote: 6. Leave travel concession. — Leave travel concession shall be admissible to the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1, in accordance with the provisions applicable to persons appointed to civil services and posts in connection with the affairs of the Union of India under the Central Civil Services (Leave Travel Concession) Rules, 1988.
sections.relevance_reason: Purely administrative, no data compliance relevance

sections.heading: वैयजिक डािा उल् लंघि की सूचिा.—(1) दकसी भी वैयजिक डािा उल् लंघि की िािकारी प्राप् त होिे पर, िेटा
sections.title: Terms and conditions of service for civil servants
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Civil Service (Conduct) Rules, 1964 apply to civil servants in the same manner as to government servants. It also requires that provisions from Part IV to Part IX of the Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965 apply to these officers and employees. Any unaddressed terms and conditions must be decided by the Central Government.

requirements.text: The provisions of the Civil Service (Conduct) Rules, 1964 shall apply to the officers and employees in like manner as applicable to a person appointed to a civil service or post

The provisions contained in Part IV to Part IX of the Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965 shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1

Any matter relating to the terms and conditions of service of the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1, in respect of which no express provision has been made in these rules, shall be referred to the Central Government for its decision

sections.source_quote: 7. Other terms and conditions of service. — (1) The provisions of the Civil Service (Conduct) Rules, 1964 shall apply to the officers and employees in like manner as applicable to a person appointed to a civil service or post in connection with the affairs of the Union of India under the said rules. (2) The provisions contained in Part IV to Part IX of the Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965 shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1, in like manner as applicable to Government servant under the said rules. (3) Any matter relating to the terms and conditions of service of the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1, in respect of which no express provision has been made in these rules, shall be referred to the Central Government for its decision, and the decision of the Central Government on the same shall be final.
sections.relevance_reason: Irrelevant: civil service conduct rules, not data protection

sections.heading: डिर्िगष्ट उद्देश्य के डलए समयावडि को अब पूरा िहीं मािा िाएर्ा.—(1) कोई िेटा द़िड्युसरी, िो उस वर्ग का है और
sections.title: Conflict of interest and financial interests for Board members
sections.summary: Board members must avoid conflicts of interest and ensure no financial interests that could impair their official duties. They must comply with Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965, for Group 'A' officers. They are prohibited from receiving sitting fees or sumptuary allowances for attending meetings. Any unaddressed service conditions must be decided by the Central Government.

requirements.text: Chairperson and every other Member shall ensure absence of conflict of interest in the performance of functions
Chairperson and every other Member shall not have any financial or other interests likely to prejudicially affect performance of functions
Provisions of Part IV to Part IX of Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965 apply mutatis mutandis to Chairperson and Members
Chairperson and every other Member shall not be entitled to any sitting fee for attending meetings
Chairperson and every other Member shall not be entitled to any sumptuary allowance
Any unaddressed conditions of service shall be referred to Central Government for final decision

sections.source_quote: 8. Other terms and conditions of service. — (1) The Chairperson and every other Member shall ensure absence of conflict of interest in the performance of the functions of her office and shall not have any such financial or other interests as are likely to prejudicially affect the performance of the functions of such office.
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential governance for board compliance

sections.heading: प्रसंथकरण के बारे में प्रश्नों का उिर िेिे के डलए व्यडि की संपकग िािकारी.—प्रत्येक िेटा द़िड्युसरी अपिी वेबसाइट या
sections.title: Definition of pay matrix for Board members
sections.summary: The term 'pay matrix' refers to the pay structure specified in Annexure I to the Central Government's Resolution published in the Official Gazette on July 25, 2016. This definition applies to the Board's terms and conditions of service.

requirements.text: In this Schedule, 'pay matrix' means the pay matrix specified in Annexure I to the Central Government's Resolution published in the Official Gazette vide Notification no. 1-2/2016-IC, dated the 25th July, 2016

sections.source_quote: 9. In this Schedule, "pay matrix" shall mean the pay matrix specified in Annexure I to the Central Government's Resolution published in the Official Gazette vide Notification no. 1-2/2016-IC, dated the 25th July, 2016.
sections.relevance_reason: pure amendment mechanics

sections.heading: बच्चे के वैजयजिक िेटा के प्रसंथकरण हेतु सत्यापि योग्य सहमडत.—(1) िेटा द़िड्युसरी यह सुडिडश्चत करिे के डलए
sections.title: Preventing conflicts of interest in Consent Manager leadership
sections.summary: The Consent Manager must implement measures to prevent conflicts of interest among its directors, key managerial personnel, and senior management who hold directorships, financial interests, employment, or beneficial ownership in Data Fiduciaries, or have material pecuniary relationships with them. This ensures leadership independence and avoids undue influence on data processing decisions. The requirement applies to Consent Managers registered under the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires Consent Managers to prevent conflicts of interest in leadership, which could affect data transfer decisions if leadership has financial ties to Data Fiduciaries. It impacts DTIA by mandating transparency in leadership structures.

requirements.text: Consent Manager must implement measures to prevent conflicts of interest among directors, key managerial personnel, and senior management
No conflict of interest arising from directors, key managerial personnel, or senior management holding directorship, financial interest, employment, or beneficial ownership in Data Fiduciaries
No conflict of interest arising from material pecuniary relationships with Data Fiduciaries

sections.source_quote: 10. The Consent Manager shall have in place measures to ensure that no conflict of interest arises on account of its directors, key managerial personnel and senior management holding a directorship, financial interest, employment or beneficial ownership in Data Fiduciaries, or having a material pecuniary relationship with them.
sections.relevance_reason: conflict of interest in consent management

sections.heading: दिव्यांर्ििों के वैजयजिक िेटा के प्रसंथकरण के डलए सत्यापि योग्य सहमडत, डििके पास वैि अडभभावक है— (1)
sections.title: Transparency requirements for Consent Manager disclosures
sections.summary: The Consent Manager must publish easily accessible information on its website or app about company promoters, directors, key managerial personnel, senior management, shareholders holding over 2% of shares, and corporate entities with significant shareholdings in the Consent Manager. This transparency requirement applies to Consent Managers registered under the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025 and aims to enhance accountability in data processing.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates transparency in Consent Manager leadership and ownership structures, which is critical for DTIA to assess potential conflicts of interest and ensure data transfer compliance with governance standards.

requirements.text: Publish easily accessible information on website or app about promoters, directors, key managerial personnel, and senior management
Publish information about shareholders holding shares exceeding two per cent of the company
Publish information about corporate entities where promoters, directors, key managerial personnel, or senior management hold shares exceeding two per cent
Publish additional information as the Board directs for transparency

sections.source_quote: 11. The Consent Manager shall publish in an easily accessible manner, on its website or app, or both, as the case may be, information regarding: — (a) the promoters, directors, key managerial personnel and senior management of the company registered as Consent Manager; (b) every person who holds shares in excess of two per cent. of the shareholding of the company registered as Consent Manager; (c) everybody corporate in whose shareholding any promoter, director, key managerial personnel or senior management of the Consent Manager holds shares in excess of two per cent as on the first day of the preceding calendar month; and (d) such other information as the Board may direct the Consent Manager to disclose in the interests of transparency.
sections.relevance_reason: Transparency for consent managers' governance

sections.heading: बच्चे के वैजयजिक िेटा के प्रसंथकरण पर लार्ू कुछ िाडयत्वों से छूट— (1) अडिडियम की िारा 9 की उप-िारा (1) और
sections.title: Audit mechanisms for Consent Manager compliance
sections.summary: The Consent Manager must establish effective audit mechanisms to review, monitor, evaluate, and report on technical and organizational controls, continued fulfillment of registration conditions, and adherence to obligations under the rules. These audits must be conducted periodically and as directed by the Board, ensuring ongoing compliance with data processing requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires regular audits of Consent Manager compliance, which is essential for DTIA to verify that data transfers adhere to governance standards and technical safeguards.

requirements.text: Implement effective audit mechanisms to review technical and organizational controls, systems, procedures, and safeguards
Monitor and evaluate continued fulfillment of registration conditions
Report audit outcomes to the Board periodically and as directed
Ensure adherence to obligations under the Act and these rules

sections.source_quote: 12. The Consent Manager shall have in place effective audit mechanisms to review, monitor, evaluate and report the outcome of such audit to the Board, periodically and on such other occasions as the Board may direct, in respect of— (a) technical and organisational controls, systems, procedures and safeguards; (b) continued fulfilment of the conditions of registration; and (c) adherence to its obligations under the Act and these rules.
sections.relevance_reason: Audit mechanisms for consent compliance

sections.heading: महत्वपूणग िेटा द़िड्युसरी के अडतररि िाडयत्व—(1) एक महत्वपूणग िेटा द़िड्युसरी, इस अडिडियम और उसके अिीि
sections.title: Restrictions on transfer of Consent Manager control
sections.summary: The control of a company registered as a Consent Manager cannot be transferred through sale, merger, or other means without prior approval from the Board and fulfillment of conditions specified by the Board. This ensures that leadership and ownership structures remain stable and compliant with data processing obligations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section restricts the transfer of Consent Manager control, which is critical for DTIA to assess the stability of data transfer mechanisms and prevent unauthorized changes to data processing structures.

requirements.text: Control of Consent Manager company cannot be transferred by sale, merger, or other means
Prior approval from the Board required for any transfer of control
Fulfillment of conditions specified by the Board required for control transfers

sections.source_quote: 13. The control of the company registered as the Consent Manager shall not be transferred by way of sale, merger or otherwise, except with the previous approval of the Board and subject to fulfilment of such conditions as the Board may specify in this behalf.
sections.relevance_reason: Consent management transfer restrictions

sections.heading: िेटा प्प्रंडसपल के अडिकार.—(1) अडिडियम के तहत िेटा प्प्रंडसपल को अपिे अडिकारों का प्रयोर् करिे में सक्षम
sections.title: Data Principals' rights to exercise data rights via website/app
sections.summary: Data Principals can exercise their rights under the Act by making requests to Data Fiduciaries via their website or app. The Data Fiduciary must prominently publish details of request means and required identifiers. Data Fiduciaries must implement technical and organizational measures to respond to requests within ninety days. Data Principals may also nominate individuals to exercise rights using the same means.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Principals' rights to access and control their data through website/app interfaces require Data Fiduciaries to implement robust systems for request handling, directly impacting data transfer assessments by mandating specific technical and procedural safeguards.

requirements.text: Data Fiduciaries and Consent Managers must prominently publish on website or app details of means for Data Principals to make requests for rights exercise.
Data Fiduciaries must publish required identifiers for Data Principals to identify themselves under terms of service.
Data Fiduciaries must implement technical and organizational measures to respond to Data Principal requests within ninety days.
Data Principals may nominate individuals to exercise rights using the same means and identifiers as specified.

sections.source_quote: For enabling Data Principals to exercise their rights under the Act, the Data Fiduciary and, where applicable, the Consent Manager, shall prominently publish on its website or app, or both, as the case may be, (a) the details of the means using which a Data Principal may make a request for the exercise of such rights; and (b) the particulars, if any, such as the username or other identifier of such a Data Principal, which may be required to identify her under its terms of service.
sections.relevance_reason: Data subject rights implementation

sections.heading: भारतीय क्षेत्राजधकार से बाहर वैजयजिक िेटा का थथािांतरण—अडिडियम के तहत िेटा द़िड्युिरी द्वारा संसाडित
sections.title: Restrictions on cross-border transfer of personal data
sections.summary: Personal data processed by a Data Fiduciary may be transferred outside India only if the Data Fiduciary meets requirements specified by the Central Government through general or special orders. These requirements apply to transfers to foreign states, entities, or agencies under their control.
sections.dtia_summary: Cross-border data transfers are restricted to cases where the Data Fiduciary complies with Central Government-specified requirements, directly impacting DTIA by mandating specific compliance checks for international transfers.

requirements.text: Personal data processed by a Data Fiduciary may be transferred outside India subject to meeting Central Government requirements.

sections.source_quote: Any personal data processed by a Data Fiduciary under the Act may be transferred outside the territory of India subject to the restriction that the Data Fiduciary shall meet such requirements as the Central Government may, by general or special order, specify in respect of making such personal data available to any foreign State, or to any person or entity under the control of or any agency of such a State.
sections.relevance_reason: Cross-border transfer restrictions core to data compliance

sections.heading: अिुसंिाि, अडभलेखीकरण या सांडख्यकीय उद्देश्यों के डलए अडिडियम से छूट— अडिडियम के प्राविाि अिुसंिाि,
sections.title: Exemption for research, archiving, and statistical purposes
sections.summary: The Act does not apply to processing personal data for research, archiving, or statistical purposes if conducted in accordance with standards specified in the Second Schedule. This exemption applies to data processing activities meeting the defined standards.
sections.dtia_summary: Research, archiving, and statistical data processing are exempt from the Act if compliant with Second Schedule standards, which may impact DTIA by allowing certain data transfers without full compliance checks.

requirements.text: The Act does not apply to processing personal data for research, archiving, or statistical purposes if carried out in accordance with Second Schedule standards.

sections.source_quote: The provisions of the Act shall not apply to the processing of personal data necessary for research, archiving or statistical purposes if it is carried on in accordance with the standards specified in Second Schedule.
sections.relevance_reason: research/exemption for data processing

sections.heading: अध्यक्ष और अन्य सिथयों की डियुडि— (1) केंद्र सरकार, अध्यक्ष के रूप में डियुडि के डलए व्यडियों की डसफाररि
sections.title: Appointment of Search-cum-Selection Committee members
sections.summary: The Central Government appoints a Search-cum-Selection Committee with the Cabinet Secretary as chairperson and other members including officials from the Ministry of Legal Affairs and Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, plus two experts. The Committee recommends individuals for appointment as Chairperson or Members.
sections.dtia_summary: The appointment process for Board members involves specific committees and expertise, which indirectly affects DTIA by ensuring regulatory oversight of data protection mechanisms.

requirements.text: The Central Government shall constitute a Search-cum-Selection Committee with the Cabinet Secretary as chairperson and Secretaries from Legal Affairs and Electronics and IT ministries as members.
The Committee shall recommend individuals for appointment as Chairperson or Members.

sections.source_quote: The Central Government shall constitute a Search-cum-Selection Committee, with the Cabinet Secretary as the chairperson and the Secretaries to the Government of India in charge of the Department of Legal Affairs and the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology and two experts of repute having special knowledge or practical experience in a field which in the opinion of the Central Government may be useful to the Board as members, to recommend individuals for appointment as Chairperson.
sections.relevance_reason: Governance for committee appointments

sections.heading: अध्यक्ष और अन्य सिथयों का वेति, भिे और सेवा की अन्य डिबंिि एवं िते.— अध्यक्ष और प्रत्येक अन्य सिथय को
sections.title: Salary and terms of service for Board members
sections.summary: The Chairperson and other Board members receive salaries and allowances as specified in the Fifth Schedule, with other terms and conditions of service also defined in the same schedule.
sections.dtia_summary: Board members' compensation and terms of service are defined in the Fifth Schedule, which indirectly affects DTIA by ensuring consistent regulatory oversight of data protection mechanisms.

requirements.text: The Chairperson and every other Member shall receive such salary and allowances and shall have such other terms and conditions of service as are specified in Fifth Schedule.

sections.source_quote: The Chairperson and every other Member shall receive such salary and allowances and shall have such other terms and conditions of service as are specified in Fifth Schedule.
sections.relevance_reason: Governance, not data compliance

sections.heading: बोिग की बैठकों की प्रदक्रया और उसके आिेिों, डििेिों और िथतावेिों का प्रमाणीकरण— (1) अध्यक्ष, बोिग की बैठकों
sections.title: Board meeting procedures and authentication
sections.summary: The Board's meetings are chaired by the Chairperson, with quorum set at one-third membership. Decisions require majority votes, with the Chairperson having a casting vote in case of equality. The Chairperson may take emergency actions and communicate them within seven days for ratification. Board orders are authenticated by the Chairperson or authorized members.
sections.dtia_summary: Board procedures ensure effective governance of data protection mechanisms, which indirectly impacts DTIA by maintaining regulatory oversight and decision-making processes for data transfers.

requirements.text: The Chairperson fixes meeting dates, times, places, and approves agendas.
Meetings are chaired by the Chairperson or another Member in her absence.
One-third of Board membership constitutes quorum for meetings.
Decisions require majority votes, with the Chairperson having a casting vote in case of equality.
Members with interest in business items cannot vote on them and must recuse themselves.
The Chairperson may take emergency actions and communicate them within seven days for ratification.
Board orders are authenticated by the Chairperson or authorized members.

sections.source_quote: The Chairperson shall fix the date, time and place of meetings of the Board, approve the items of agenda therefor, and cause notice specifying the same to be issued under her signature or that of such other individual as the Chairperson may authorise by general or special order in writing.
sections.relevance_reason: tangential governance for data compliance

sections.heading: बोिग का डिडिटल कायागलय के रूप में कायग करिा—बोिग एक डिडिटल कायागलय के रूप में कायग करेर्ा, दकसी भी
sections.title: Board operates as digital office without physical presence
sections.summary: The Board must function as a digital office using techno-legal measures to conduct proceedings without requiring physical presence of individuals. This includes the authority to summon and examine persons on oath digitally. The Board retains traditional powers to enforce attendance and oath examinations through digital means.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes digital procedural requirements for the Board, which may impact how data transfers are handled through digital channels without physical presence.

requirements.text: The Board shall function as a digital office without prejudice to its power to summon and enforce attendance of any person and examine her on oath
The Board may adopt techno-legal measures to conduct proceedings without requiring physical presence of any individual

sections.source_quote: 20. Functioning of Board as digital office. — The Board shall function as a digital office, without prejudice to its power to summon and enforce the attendance of any person and examine her on oath, may adopt techno-legal measures to conduct proceedings in a manner that does not require physical presence of any individual.
sections.relevance_reason: digital proceedings without physical presence

sections.heading: बोिग के अडिकाररयों और कमगचाररयों की डियुडि और सेवा की ितें—(1) बोिग, केन्द्रीय सरकार के पूवग अिुमोिि से,
sections.title: Board appoints officers with Central Government approval
sections.summary: The Board may appoint officers and employees with prior Central Government approval for efficient functioning. Terms and conditions of service must be specified in the Sixth Schedule. This section governs the appointment process and service conditions for Board personnel.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines appointment procedures for Board officers, which may affect how data handling roles are structured and managed within the digital framework.

requirements.text: The Board may appoint officers and employees with previous approval of the Central Government
Terms and conditions of service of Board officers and employees shall be specified in Sixth Schedule

sections.source_quote: 21. Terms and conditions of appointment and service of officers and employees of Board.— (1) The Board may, with previous approval of the Central Government, appoint such officers and employees as it may deem necessary for the efficient discharge of its functions under the provisions of the Act. (2) The terms and conditions of service of officers and employees of the Board shall be such as are specified in Sixth Schedule.

sections.title: Terms and conditions of service for civil servants
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Civil Service (Conduct) Rules, 1964 apply to civil servants in the same manner as to government servants. It also requires that provisions from Part IV to Part IX of the Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965 apply to these officers and employees. Any unaddressed terms and conditions must be decided by the Central Government.

requirements.text: The provisions of the Civil Service (Conduct) Rules, 1964 shall apply to the officers and employees in like manner as applicable to a person appointed to a civil service or post

The provisions contained in Part IV to Part IX of the Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965 shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1

Any matter relating to the terms and conditions of service of the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1, in respect of which no express provision has been made in these rules, shall be referred to the Central Government for its decision

sections.source_quote: 7. Other terms and conditions of service. — (1) The provisions of the Civil Service (Conduct) Rules, 1964 shall apply to the officers and employees in like manner as applicable to a person appointed to a civil service or post in connection with the affairs of the Union of India under the said rules. (2) The provisions contained in Part IV to Part IX of the Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965 shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1, in like manner as applicable to Government servant under the said rules. (3) Any matter relating to the terms and conditions of service of the officers and employees appointed under clause (1) of paragraph 1, in respect of which no express provision has been made in these rules, shall be referred to the Central Government for its decision, and the decision of the Central Government on the same shall be final.

sections.title: Conflict of interest and financial interests for Board members
sections.summary: Board members must avoid conflicts of interest and ensure no financial interests that could impair their official duties. They must comply with Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965, for Group 'A' officers. They are prohibited from receiving sitting fees or sumptuary allowances for attending meetings. Any unaddressed service conditions must be decided by the Central Government.

Chairperson and every other Member shall not be entitled to any sitting fee for attending meetings
Chairperson and every other Member shall not be entitled to any sumptuary allowance
Any unaddressed conditions of service shall be referred to Central Government for final decision

sections.source_quote: 8. Other terms and conditions of service. — (1) The Chairperson and every other Member shall ensure absence of conflict of interest in the performance of the functions of her office and shall not have any such financial or other interests as are likely to prejudicially affect the performance of the functions of such office.

requirements.text: Use of personal data of a Data Principal in the interest of sovereignty and integrity of India or security of the State

Such officer of the State or of any of its instrumentalities notified under clause (a) of sub-section (2) of section 17 of the Act, as the Central Government or the head of such instrumentalities, as the case may be, may designate in this behalf

sections.source_quote: 1. Use, by the State or any of its instrumentalities, of personal data of a Data Principal in the interest of sovereignty and integrity of India or security of the State. Such officer of the State or of any of its instrumentalities notified under clause (a) of sub-section (2) of section 17 of the Act, as the Central Government or the head of such instrumentalities, as the case may be, may designate in this behalf.
sections.relevance_reason: State use for sovereignty/security is core exception

sections.heading: Use, by the State or any of its instrumentalities, of
sections.title: State use of personal data for legal functions
sections.summary: This section permits the State or its instrumentalities to use personal data of a Data Principal for performing functions under any law in India or fulfilling obligations under any law in India. The authorized persons are those designated under applicable law.

requirements.text: Use of personal data of a Data Principal for performance of any function under any law for the time being in force in India
Use of personal data of a Data Principal for disclosure of any information for fulfilling any obligation under any law for the time being in force in India
Person authorized under applicable law

sections.source_quote: 2. Use, by the State or any of its instrumentalities, of personal data of a Data Principal for the following purposes, namely: — (i) performance of any function under any law for the time being in force in India; or (ii) disclosure of any information for fulfilling any obligation under any law for the time being in force in India. Person authorised under applicable law.
sections.relevance_reason: State use of personal data for legal functions

sections.heading: Carrying out assessment for notifying any Data
sections.title: Assessment for significant data fiduciaries
sections.summary: This section requires the Central Government, specifically the Secretary in charge of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, to assess and notify any Data Fiduciary or class of Data Fiduciaries as Significant Data Fiduciaries. The assessment is conducted by designated officers within the Ministry.

requirements.text: Carrying out assessment for notifying any Data Fiduciary or class of Data Fiduciaries as Significant Data Fiduciary
Such officer of the Central Government, in the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, as the Secretary in charge of the said Ministry may designate in this behalf

sections.source_quote: 3. Carrying out assessment for notifying any Data Fiduciary or class of Data Fiduciaries as Significant Data Fiduciary. Such officer of the Central Government, in the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, as the Secretary in charge of the said Ministry may designate in this behalf.
sections.relevance_reason: Core: Significant Data Fiduciary assessment for cross-border data transfers
